rulings.Case Title: BRIAN MCVEIGH VS. SF RECYCLING & DISPOSAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Plaintiffs Post-Trial Interrogatories Set No. One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF BRIAN MCVEIGH'S Motion To Compel Further Answers To Plaintiffs Post-Trial Interrogatories Set No. One. The Court adopts the report and recommendation of the judge pro tem in full. Plaintiff's motion to compel further answers to post-trial interrogatories nos. 1-6, 7, 9, and 10 is granted as set forth in the report. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The order should attach the report. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2019, Line 2, CROSS-DEFENDANT ESWindows, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant ESWindows, LLC's demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint is overruled. A party alleging an alter ego theory is required to allege only ultimate rather than evidentiary facts. "Moreover, the 'less particularity [of pleading] is required where the defendant may be assumed to possess knowledge of the facts at least equal, if not superior, to that possessed by the plaintiff,' which certainly is the case here." (Rutherford Holdings, LLC v. Plaza Del Rey (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 221, 235-236 [holding that plaintiff made sufficient allegations of alter ego to avoid a demurrer].) In addition, the first amended cross-complaint adequately pleads direct claims against ESWindows as one of two cross-defendants. (E.g., FACC Paras. 8-14, 15-18, 36-39, 43-49, 72-73.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Bagatelos is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Niesar And Vestal Llp, Gerald Niesar, And Oscar Escobars Renewed Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiff Buenofrio, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS GERALD NIESAR, OSCAR ESCOBAR, NIESAR & VESTAL, LLP'S Renewed MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff Buenofrio, Inc. (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of tentative ruling only) Defendants Niesar & Vestal LLP, Gerald Niesar, and Oscar Escobar's motion for summary judgment on Buenofrio Inc.'s claims is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether "but for the alleged malpractice, it is more likely than not that the plaintiff would have obtained a more favorable result." (Viner v. Sweet (2003) 30 Cal.4th 1232, 1244 (emphasis in original).) There is a dispute of fact whether the Monogram deal was better and whether defendants were negligent by not pursuing it. Under the Monogram proposal, Monogram was to pay over $16 million and Mr. Gutierrez would have received $4 million. (Plaintiffs' Evidence, Ex. YYYYYY.) Under the Canum Asset Purchase Agreement, Canum paid approximately $10 million and Mr. Gutierrez received $1.3 million. (Defendants' UMF 62.) The Vickery declaration indicates that PLM should have been sold for substantially more than the $10.1 million purchase price. (Vickery Decl. Para. 8.) There is also a dispute whether the Monogram deal could have been consummated. There is evidence that CVF assumed without an investigation that Monogram did not have adequate funding. (Blanco Depo., 213:1-214:17.) Defendants fail to show that Buenofrio Inc. lacks standing to maintain this attorney malpractice action because it assigned its claims. The general rule is that attorney malpractice claims are not assignable based on public policy reasons. (See, e.g., Kracht v. Perrin, Gartland & Doyle (1990) 219 Cal.App.3d 1019, 1022-1025; Jackson v. Rogers & Wells (1989) 210 Cal.App.3d 336, 341-350; Goodley v. Wank & Wank, Inc. (1976) 62 Cal.App.3d 389, 393-398.) In White Mountains Reinsurance Company of America v. Borton Petrini, LLP (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 890, the court recognized a "narrow exception" to that rule where "(1) the assignment of the legal malpractice claim is only a small, incidental part of a larger commercial transfer between insurance companies; (2) the larger transfer is of assets, rights, obligations, and liabilities and does not treat the legal malpractice claim as a distinct commodity; (3) the transfer is not to a former adversary; (4) the legal malpractice claim arose under circumstances where the original client insurance company retained the attorney to represent and defend an insured; and (5) the communications between the attorney and the original client insurance company were conducted via a third party claims administrator." (Id. at 892.) Those factors are not present in the instant case. (See Busbice v. Vuckovich (N.D. Ill. Dec. 1, 2018) WL 6272047 at *3 ["[I]t is not clear whether the exception would ever extend beyond assignments between insurance companies. Courts interpreting White Mountains have either interpreted the exception as applying solely to insurance companies or recognized as a favorable factor a client's status as an insurance company"].) (Tentative Ruling Continues in Part 2 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff Ignacio Gutierrez
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS GERALD NIESAR, OSCAR ESCOBAR, AND NIESAR & VESTAL, LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff Ignacio Gutierrez. Defendants Niesar & Vestal LLP, Gerald Niesar, and Oscar Escobar's motion for summary judgment on Ignacio Gutierrez's claims is denied. Defendants fail to show that Mr. Gutierrez's malpractice claim is time-barred per Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 340.6. Defendants do not establish as a matter of law that Mr. Gutierrez incurred "actual injury" on February 24, 2014, when the voting trust was created. Rather, the record shows that he sustained actual injury on April 1, 2016, when the Asset Purchase Agreement was executed and he allegedly received an inadequate purchase price. (Defendants' UMF 68, 73.) (See, e.g., Jordache Enterprises, Inc. v. Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison (1998) 18 Cal.4th 739, 765 [for purposes of Sec. 340.6, "actual injury occurs when the plaintiff sustains any loss or injury legally cognizable as damages in a legal malpractice action based on the acts or omissions that the plaintiff alleged"]; Callahan v. Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 557, 573-575 [defective provisions in limited partnership agreement caused only contingent harm until general partner became incapacitated].) Plaintiff timely filed this action within one year of that date. The court concludes that there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Mr. Gutierrez could have obtained a better result but for defendants' malpractice as explained in the companion motion. Further, Defendants concede that the Monogram deal would have provided additional funds for Mr. Gutierrez. (Reply at 3, 6.) Defendants' arguments regarding disgorgement and unfair competition standing fail as they do not completely dispose of all of Mr. Gutierrez's claims and defendants do not seek summary adjudication. Lastly, the court finds that Mr. Escobar is not entitled to summary judgment. There is a dispute of fact regarding whether the parties intended to waive claims as to him. The body of the letter agreement indicates that Mr. Gutierrez was waiving claims as to PLM and its affiliates only. He was not waiving claims against his attorneys. In fact, the letter agreement expressly preserves claims against "Niesar & Vestal, LLP." Mr. Escobar was a partner of Niesar & Vestal, LLP. (FACC Para. 3.) The waiver is reasonably susceptible to Mr. Gutierrez's interpretation that his claims against Mr. Escobar were preserved. (See Gutierrez Decl., Para. 42; Wolf v. Superior Court (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1343, 1351 [explaining that the trier of fact resolves meaning of ambiguous contract where the parole evidence is disputed].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Niesar And Vestal Llp, Gerald Niesar, And Oscar Escobars Renewed Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiff Buenofrio, Inc. (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2019, Line 3, Defendants Niesar And Vestal Llp, Gerald Niesar, And Oscar Escobars Renewed Motion For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiff Buenofrio, Inc. (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only) (Continued from Part 1 of 2) Defendant fails to show that Buenofrio Inc. waived its claims. Buenofrio, Inc. agreed to waive claims against "the Business, the Acquired Assets, Purchaser, any of Purchaser's affiliates . . . , and agreed not to bring any actions or claims "against or adverse to the Business, the Acquired Asets, Purchaser, [or] any of Purchaser's affiliates . . . ." (Asset Purchase Agreement, Sec. 6.11(a).) Defendants do not fall within any of the above categories. Further, the last sentence of Sec. 6.11 provides that plaintiffs "shall not release any rights and claims arising from or in connection with this Agreement . . . ." Plaintiff's claims are in connection with the Asset Purchase Agreement as they assert that it never should have been executed. Defendants' arguments regarding the unfair competition and disgorgement claims are irrelevant as defendants did not seek summary adjudication in the alternative. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (This is the end of the tentative ruling.) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DAN SCHUMAN ET AL VS. PETER CHIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Fin. Discovery - Notice, Mpa And Atty. Decl. - Schuman V. Chin.Pdf
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS DAN SCHUMAN AND KATSUE SCHUMAN'S Motion for Order Permitting Discovery of Defendant's Profits and Financial Condition. Pro Tem Judge Sigrid Irias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for order permitting discovery of defendant's profits and financial condition denied without prejudice to raising the issue before the trial judge. The evidence submitted does not establish a substantial probability that the plaintiffs will prevail on their claim for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sigrid.irias@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AKANE SHIBA VS. GEORGE N. MAINAS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2019, Line 6, CROSS-DEFENDANT ANDREA DAVIS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant Andrea Davis's demurrer to Cross-Complainants George N. Mainas and Maria K. Mainas' first amended cross-complaint is overruled as to the first, second, and third causes of action. Although the demurrer was filed one day late, the Court exercises its discretion to consider it on its merits. (McAllister v. County of Monterey (2007) 147 Cal.App.3d 253, 280.) (The Court's full tentative ruling has been e-mailed to the parties.) rulings.Case Title: SELENA SCOLA VS. ISLAND PARK STORAGE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANTS ISLAND PARK STORAGE, AND BIZON GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Island Park Storage and Bizon Group, Inc.'s demurrer to Plaintiff Selena Scola's complaint is off calendar for failure to meet and confer. (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 430.41.) Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41 requires the demurring party to "meet and confer in person or by telephone" with the party who filed the complaint before a demurrer is filed. Counsel for Defendants failed to file a declaration to indicate that such a meet and confer effort took place before he filed the demurrer. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendants have until September 5, 2019 to respond to the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)
